meaning: adj.无确定路线,不稳定的,奇怪的n.古怪的人,漂泊无定的人
examples: The electricity supply here is quite erratic.
Businessmen are displeased with erratic economic policy making.
Heart rate tracking for three of the Alaska bears showed a drop from 55 beats per minute on average before hibernation to 14 erratic ones per minute in winter.
The stock owner should not be too concerned with erratic fluctuations in stock prices.
The world is a more remarkable place when women do erratic and exciting things.
